description:
GOAL: Inline non-tail calls — `Term::Call(F, args) -> Cont{c, captured}` where
F is inlinable. F's Return becomes a TailCall to the continuation FnIr.

Parent: fz-ul4.11.15. Depends on: fz-ul4.11.15.2.

## Deliverables (extend src/ir_inline.rs)

  /// One pass over the module. For each fn C, for each block B with a
  /// Call(F, args) -> Cont{K, captured} terminator where F.id != C.id and
  /// is_inlinable(F), rewrite via splice. Returns total rewrites performed.
  pub fn inline_calls_once(m: &mut Module) -> usize;

## Rewrite recipe

  Given: caller C, block B with terminator `Call { callee: F, args,
  continuation: Cont { fn_id: K, captured } }`. F, K, C all in module m.

  1. Preconditions: F.id != C.id; is_inlinable(F).
  2. (F', vmap, bmap) = alpha_rename(F, &C).
  3. Walk F'.blocks; rewrite each terminator:
       Return(v')  ->  TailCall { callee: K, args: [v', captured...] }
                       The captured list comes from B's ORIGINAL Cont and uses
                       caller-local Var ids (these are NOT remapped by
                       alpha_rename because they live in C, not F').
       Halt(v')    ->  unchanged.
       Goto/If     ->  unchanged.
       Other Term variants CANNOT appear — F is leaf.
  4. F'_entry = splice_blocks(&mut C, F').
  5. B's terminator becomes `Goto(F'_entry, args)`.

## Why TailCall(K, [v, captured...]) is semantically equivalent

  ir_interp.rs:128-134 (Term::Call semantics):
      let result = run(ctx, *callee, arg_vals)?;
      let cap_vals = collect_vals(&env, &continuation.captured)?;
      let mut cont_args = vec![result];
      cont_args.extend(cap_vals);
      return run(ctx, continuation.fn_id, cont_args);

  Our rewrite produces TailCall(K, [v, captured...]). The trampoline's
  TailCall handling (codegen.rs:2185) calls K with those same arg slots.
  K's entry params are [result, cap_params...] (set up by cps_split_call in
  ir_lower.rs:684-690). Identical operational result.

  The cont-frame machinery in emit_call (codegen.rs:2129) is bypassed in
  favor of the normal TailCall path — that's strictly fewer allocations.

## Captured-var lifetime

  The Vars in B's original Cont.captured were defined by stmts in C BEFORE
  block B, and are live at B. They remain live at the splice-injected
  Goto(F'_entry, args) (no new defs interpose). They are still live at the
  rewritten TailCall(K, [v, captured]) inside F's body because:
    - F' uses fresh Vars only (alpha_rename shifted past C's max).
    - F' is a contiguous block-chain entered from B; no other caller mutates
      these slots in between.
  So the captured Vars are well-defined at the TailCall site. Verify in tests
  via ir_interp parity.

## Tests

  - Hand-built: `inc(x) = x + 1` (leaf, 2 stmts). Caller with
    `Call(inc, [x]) -> Cont{K, [y]}`. After pass: caller's term =
    `Goto(spliced_entry, [x])`; spliced inc-body computes `x+1`; spliced
    Return becomes `TailCall(K, [sum, y])`.
  - Self-recursion: not inlined.
  - Non-leaf: not inlined.
  - Over-budget: not inlined.
  - ir_interp parity: hand-built module where main does
    `print(double(3))` shaped as Call+Cont; pre- and post-inline same Value
    sequence.
  - Multiple inlinable Calls in one caller.

## Out of scope

  - Pipeline integration (.15.5 wires this in).
  - Closure call shapes (.15.4).

## Acceptance

  - cargo test passes.
  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.15.3.

## References

  - fz-ul4.11.15 architecture section, Case 2.
  - ir_interp.rs:128-134 (Call semantics).
  - ir_lower.rs:664-700 (cps_split_call; cont entry-param layout).
title: Inline Call+Cont sites (Case 2)
